_description: Actual match outcomes and per-question RBP results from the JTC SportsPredict platform

question_results.text: Will Kylian Mbappé (France) score a goal?
Will Ousmane Dembélé (France) have 2 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Second-biggest win of the match. Priced off his bimodal SOT pattern (blanked vs tougher opposition) rather than a naive empirical average.

question_results.text: Will Julio Enciso (Paraguay) have 1 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: Deliberately did NOT re-run the suppression play that lost -26.3 RBP on this exact player 6 days earlier (GER-PAR); trusted the market instead. Correct call.

question_results.text: Will the match have 3 or more total goals?
Will Miguel Almirón (Paraguay) score or assist?
question_results.note: PERSONAL_ZERO_OVERRIDES_TEAM_CONTEXT: 0 goals/assists/SOT in every game he played, discounted well below crowd.

question_results.text: Will France have 7 or more shots on target?
question_results.note: The one clear miss of the match. Blended 45% market/55% empirical toward France's rising SOT trend (season-high 12 in the R32 game) -- reasonable regime-coverage logic, but still landed on the wrong side. Crowd's lower 58% was closer. A real, if much smaller-scale, echo of the CAN-MOR Q11 mechanism (empirical-lambda blend pushing above the market) -- worth flagging even though the magnitude here was contained (-15.7 vs -80.83).

question_results.text: Will France score in both halves?
Will there be 4 or more total cards shown?
Will a substitute score a goal?
Will France have 6 or more corner kicks?
Will the match be tied at halftime?
Will Paraguay hold a lead at any point?
question_results.note: No-market question anchored off the Team-to-score-first price (14.3%) plus a small addition for late-lead scenarios; landed close to crowd and correct.

question_results.text: Will Michael Olise (France) score or assist?
Will a penalty kick be awarded?

key_lessons: Best match of the recent stretch: 13/15 beat crowd, only 2 small misses (-15.7, -3.97), no catastrophic loss.
Biggest win: Dembélé 2+ SOT (+31.83) -- trusted a bimodal, opponent-quality-dependent read over a flat market/empirical average.

The Enciso call (+10.3) is the cleanest validation of learning from a named prior loss: same player, same question type, same underlying data pattern as a -26.3 RBP loss 6 days earlier on GER-PAR -- this time the suppression temptation was deliberately NOT repeated, and it worked.

The one miss (France 7+ SOT, -15.7) is a smaller-scale echo of the CAN-MOR catastrophe: blending toward an empirical SOT average pushed the estimate above the market again, and again the market was closer -- though the damage was contained because the blend was more conservative (55% vs CAN-MOR's 80%) and the underlying signal had genuine regime coverage. Still lost. Worth tracking as a continued data point against TEAM_SOT_EMPIRICAL_FLOOR in ANY direction, not just the aggressive-blend failure mode.
